purpose: Input fixture for the Mode 3 prune formula oracle. The harness at oracle-harness/run.sh feeds these input tuples into the upstream Scala FullBlockPruningProcessor and emits runtime-vectors.json, which is the ground truth the Rust parity test asserts against. This file also indexes intentional Rust-vs-Scala divergences (see intentional_rust_divergences + divergent_vectors).

intentional_rust_divergences.name: archive_arm_no_reset
intentional_rust_divergences.scala_behavior: blocks_to_keep<0 AND utxo_bootstrap=false writes GenesisHeight (=1), resetting any prior sentinel
intentional_rust_divergences.rust_behavior: blocks_to_keep<0 keeps current_min unchanged, never walking backward
intentional_rust_divergences.rationale: Scala's reset would allow post-eviction resurrection on a previously-bootstrapped store toggled back to archive. Rust's never-walk-backward is a monotonicity invariant the storage / receive / serve / rollback gates depend on.

intentional_rust_divergences.name: suffix_snap_no_walk_back
intentional_rust_divergences.scala_behavior: blocks_to_keep>0 + candidate>VL: writes min(candidate, candidate-(candidate%VL)) = snapped_value, even when snapped < current_min
intentional_rust_divergences.rust_behavior: writes snapped.max(current_min), preserving the sentinel when an aggressive epoch snap would walk it backward
intentional_rust_divergences.rationale: Scala can move the prune sentinel backward across the voting-epoch boundary. That would unprune blocks mid-sync. Rust's monotonicity floor blocks the regression.

intentional_rust_divergences.name: headers_only_does_not_advance_sentinel
intentional_rust_divergences.scala_behavior: blocks_to_keep == 0 falls through to the suffix-window math (the gate is `< 0`, not `<= 0`); for header_height=200 the formula returns 201 even though Mode 6 nodes never apply full blocks
intentional_rust_divergences.rationale: Mode 6 (canonical headers-only) is the only legal blocks_to_keep=0 configuration in our runtime. Mode 6 nodes never apply full blocks, so the only callers of this formula with blocks_to_keep=0 are defensive paths. Advancing the sentinel for a non-existent full-block apply would silently arm storage gates against headers-only state.

convergent_vectors.description: Scala's archive arm splits on utxo_bootstrap: when true, returns readMinimalFullBlockHeight() (pinned). Rust collapses both archive sub-arms to current_min, so this case converges.

divergent_vectors.description: Scala resets archive sentinel to 1; Rust keeps current_min
Scala snaps below current_min; Rust monotonicity guard keeps current_min. Math: candidate = max(5000, 5100-999) = 5000; snap = 5000 - (5000 % 1024) = 5000 - 904 = 4096.
Scala's suffix-window math fires for blocks_to_keep=0 and emits 201; Rust's explicit early return keeps current_min
